post.content:
## Profile

Current status: 5 years of professional experience
Position: Backend/Full-stack developer
Education: Masters in computer science from a top tier school in India

**Interview experience:**

**Technical phone screen**

I was asked one single [question](https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/398642/Uber-or-Phone-Screen-or-Merge-Sorted-Interval-Lists) on the phone which i was able to solve completely with all the edge cases and 5 mins remaining for questions for the interviewers.

*Feedback received:*

+ve: Good coding and communication skills
-ve:  Too eager to jump into coding

**Onsite interviews**

**1. Bar raiser**

This round was mainly about my past projects and how i managed the constraints in those projects including the deadlines and helping the junior engineers in the team. The discussions regarding my projects were quite in depth. In this round uber looks for the below things:

1. How you make decisions regarding a project.
2. How in-depth is your technical and business understanding of the projects you have worked on.
3. How well are you able to communicate complex ideas in a simple manner, basically technical communication.

**2. System design**

The problem was to design a **2 player game** for the browser environment (cannot give the details). Below were the steps i took to design the game.

1. Discussed all possible scenarios that could occur during the game play, so that the rules and constraints are clear.
2. Drew a high level overview of the system components.
3. Designed the backend in more detail including APIs, type of requests, type of data storage etc.
4. Designed the frontend of the game in more detail including the component design for the game

I felt this was the round where i did best.

**3. Coding problem 1**

You are given a blob of text and you have to split the text into parts of 160 characters each just like twitter does for its' tweets and each of those parts were required to be formatted with a prefix like `1/3`, meaning part 1 of total 3 parts. The prefix is also included in the character limit.

Follow up: Design the algorithm for a text which has millions of words.

I was able to write a working code for the first part, the code needed to be compiled and i also wrote some unit-tests for the edge cases. For the follow up, there wasn't much time left so we just discussed the approach and complexity. 

**4. Coding problem 2**

For this problem i had to write code using `HTML` and `JS`. As far as remember, the task was to build a grid, where a grid can contain other smaller grids and so on. The data for the grid was provided as a response of a network request. So you parse the data, traverse the child grids and plot the structure using whatever `HTML` and `CSS` knowledge you have.

I was stumped with this problem as i did not have enough practice in building front-ends in a time constrained manner but i didn't give up and was able to come up with a decent looking front-end.

**5. Hiring manager interview**

The interviewer digged into my past projects, asked questions regarding the technical concepts involved in my past projects and gave some `what-if` scenarios regarding those projects.

**Result**

Just few days after the onsite the recruiter contacted me regarding the positive decision.

**Thank you leetcode and it's great community!!**

6-7 months back i could not even imagine the possibility of clearing the Uber rounds but with hard-work and luck anything is possible.

I did the below things for my prep:

* I was honest with my preparation and i aimed at filling the gaps i had in my knowledge.
* I read other people's solution to the problem i solved, read the interview experiences to analyse what i needed to cover.
* A month before the onsite i started solving uber focused problems to feel confident for the D'day.


Good luck everyone!

What was the timeline for your interview process? I am curious to know how much time you can ask for between the recruiter interview and the phone screen, as well as how much time you can ask for between the phone screen and the onsite.

Congratulations [@carl-sagan](https://leetcode.com/carl-sagan) . I am also a UI engineer and preparing for tier 1 companies so just wanted to ask that in UI role the main emphasis is on javascript and building FE in a time constraint, so how to be good at it ? How to prepare for system design rounds? Thanks
Hi Carl, congrats. Can you please guide how did you prepare for system design?\n
Congratulations and wish you all the best :)
